scenario_name: Synthetic-data generation pipeline using real PII for downstream model training
description: An organization runs a pipeline that uses real customer data as seed to generate synthetic equivalents for downstream model training and analytics. The promise of synthetic data is privacy preservation; the documented risk is that re-identification, membership inference, and attribute disclosure remain feasible against many naive synthesis approaches. The pipeline must be assessed against DSGAI10 (synthetic-data pitfalls) and DSGAI18 (inference and reconstruction).
assumptions: Synthesis runs inside a privacy-controlled enclave; raw data does not leave.
Synthetic outputs are intended to enable training and analytics outside the enclave.
Generator model is not retrained on synthetic outputs (avoiding distribution-drift).

components.description: Self-hosted generative model for text synthesis.
Audit log of every synthesis run with metadata about the seed and outputs.

risks_identified.rationale: Synthetic data is presumed safe to share; in practice re-identification, attribute inference, and singling-out remain feasible on many synthesis approaches.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Re-identification auditor runs on every synthesis batch; pipeline blocks releases exceeding the threshold.

risks_identified.rationale: Membership inference against the generator may identify seed records; embedding inversion against text generators recovers source content.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Membership-inference audit on every release candidate; threshold-gated.

risks_identified.rationale: Synthetic data fed into downstream training propagates errors and biases; poisoning of seed data poisons everything downstream.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Seed-data integrity checks pre-synthesis; not all biases captured.

risks_identified.rationale: Lineage between seed records, synthesis runs, and downstream models must be traceable for DSAR/RTBF.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Lineage captured for tabular synthesis; text-synthesis lineage partial.

risks_identified.rationale: GDPR/CCPA implications: synthetic-data status is contested; some regulators treat synthetic data as derived from personal data and still in scope.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Legal opinion on file; processor terms updated; DSAR pipeline traces seed records.

risks_identified.rationale: Audit logs contain seed-record references and synthesis prompts; sensitive.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Logs inside the enclave; access role-gated.

existing_controls.description: Re-identification auditor on every synthesis batch; threshold-gated release.
Membership-inference audit on each release candidate.
Differential-privacy noise injection on tabular synthesis (where applicable).
Lineage capture for synthesis runs (seed → synthesis → output IDs).
Audit logs inside the privacy-controlled enclave with role-gated access.

control_gaps.description: Text-synthesis lineage is partial; DSAR cannot always trace a seed record back from downstream outputs.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Adopt per-record provenance tags through the text-synthesis path; align with DSAR pipeline.

control_gaps.description: Differential-privacy noise budgets are configured per pipeline run but not centrally tracked.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Centralize epsilon tracking across runs; document the org-wide privacy-loss budget.

control_gaps.description: Generator-model retraining on synthetic outputs is policy-prohibited but not technically enforced.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Tag every synthesized record so downstream training pipelines can detect and refuse synthetic-on-synthetic loops.

framework_alignments.control_name: Data protection by design and default
